pull.title: qa: Support `get_bind_addrs` and `feature_bind_extra` on illumos
pull.body:
This PR is a follow-up to #34256 and extends support for `get_bind_addrs` and `feature_bind_extra` to illumos-based OSes.

Here are CI runs:
- OmniOS: https://github.com/hebasto/bitcoin-core-nightly/actions/runs/25391966720/job/74468553196
- OpenIndiana: https://github.com/hebasto/bitcoin-core-nightly/actions/runs/25391966733/job/74468552823

Addresses [this](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34256#issuecomment-4361855749) comment.

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Code Coverage & Benchmarks For details see: https://corecheck.dev/bitcoin/bitcoin/pulls/35216. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | Concept ACK | [fanquake](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35216#issuecomment-4543327042) | | Approach ACK | [l0rinc](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35216#pullrequestreview-4581284102) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore.
Do we also need to trim the CIDR suffix, see https://github.com/hebasto/bitcoin-core-nightly/actions/runs/25427210121/job/74583785372#step:9:501?

Concept ACK - seems fine given such a small diff. Why is it a draft?

> Do we also need to trim the CIDR suffix, see https://github.com/hebasto/bitcoin-core-nightly/actions/runs/25427210121/job/74583785372#step:9:501?

Seems like the NetBSD tests were also broken; should include those changes here?

> Why is it a draft?
> 
> Seems like the NetBSD tests were also broken; should include those changes here?

There are a few points to do:
1. Check the fixed tests on NetBSD locally.
2. Update the build documentation to mention the lsof package where needed.

qa: Add `skip_if_no_lsof` helper and use it where needed

Some functional tests on non-Linux platforms rely on the `lsof` utility.
However, we treat all other functional test dependencies, such as
additional Python modules, as optional, and skip dependent tests if
those are unavailable.

This change makes `lsof` optional as well.

qa: Strip prefix length from NetBSD `ifconfig` output

Modern NetBSD `ifconfig` prints interface addresses in CIDR notation
(e.g. `inet 127.0.0.1/8`), unlike the other supported platforms which
print the netmask as a separate field. The trailing prefix length breaks
functional tests that expect a plain IP address.

This change is a no-op on other platforms.

events.message:

qa: Ignore `lsof` warnings on NetBSD

On NetBSD, `lsof` can produce the following warnings:
1. "created device cache file: ..." - usually happens in a CI
environment.
2. "compiled for NetBSD release 10.0; this is 10.1." or similar - when
the `lsof` binary package is installed on a point release.

This change suppresses both warnings printed to stderr and fixes the
affected tests.

events.message:

the `lsof` binary package is installed on a point release.

This change suppresses both warnings printed to stderr and fixes the
affected tests.

events.body:
The OpenIndiana logs show IPv6 bind failing (so it's likely not finished yet), but the change still makes sense, thanks for continuing the work on this.

approach ACK, reviewed 943b232cf0912597bf8252f518489930166fa27b

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -1027,6 +1027,11 @@ def skip_if_platform_not_linux(self):
         if platform.system() != "Linux":
             raise SkipTest("not on a Linux system")
 
+    def skip_if_no_lsof(self):
+        """Skip the running test if the lsof utility is not available on non-Linux platforms."""
+        if sys.platform != "linux" and shutil.which("lsof") is None:
comments.body:
> [b3a71f5](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35216/changes/b3a71f577fb66c43432e3fee941e2b296001f781) _qa: Add `skip_if_no_lsof` helper and use it where needed_:
nit: the `sys.platform != "linux"` part indicates the name should probably be adjusted.
